title: Hidden Talents

pages.text: Once, a tiny lion cub was lost and abandoned in the deep forest.
A kind mother goat saw the helpless cub and brought him home to grow up with her own kids.
The cub watched the other kids and grew up believing he was a goat-kid too.
He bleated, ate green grass, and played happily just like the goat-kids.
In his mind, the lion cub was entirely like a goat.
One day, a wild adult lion passed by and was shocked to see this cub bleating like a goat.
The big lion caught the cub and said, "Hey cub, you're not a goat-kid, you're a lion!" But the cub didn't believe him.
The next day, the big lion caught the cub again. The cub bleated in fear and cried, "Oh Sir, please don't kill me!"
"You foolish cub, come with me and I will show you who you really are," the big lion said, taking him to a river to look at his reflection.
The cub looked into the water, realized who he was, and learned how to roar instead of bleat.
The big lion smiled and said, "Just because you grew up with goats, you can't be a goat. Realize your true power!"

moral: Believe in yourself and discover your hidden talents. Everyone has unique strengths waiting to be uncovered.
